name: X: Home Timeline Digest
description: Open the authenticated X home timeline in a fresh tab, scroll a bounded number of cards, and return a structured digest of visible posts. Use when you need a quick read of what is on the logged-in user's For You / Following feed right now. Do not use to inspect a specific author (prefer `x_profile_posts`), or to mutate anything.

browser_automation.description: Opens https://x.com/home in a fresh tab so parallel runs don't clobber each other. Scrolls up to 8 times to accumulate ~30 cards, then returns a compact digest.

sequences.name: x_home_timeline_digest
sequences.description: Open X home -> wait for timeline cards -> scroll to load more -> extract digest.

steps.name: Open the X home timeline in a fresh tab
Allow the home timeline to hydrate
Wait for timeline post cards
steps.selector: main article[data-testid='tweet'], main [data-testid='cellInnerDiv'] article, main article

steps.name: Scroll until enough timeline cards are visible
steps.item_selector: main article[data-testid='tweet'], main [data-testid='cellInnerDiv'] article, main article

steps.name: Read a digest of visible timeline posts
steps.script: const normalize = (value) => (value || '').replace(/\u00a0/g, ' ').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim(); const normalizeUrl = (raw) => { if (!raw) return ''; try { return new URL(raw, window.location.origin).toString(); } catch { return String(raw); } }; const getStatusUrl = (article) => { const links = Array.from(article.querySelectorAll("a[href*='/status/']")).map((link) => normalizeUrl(link.getAttribute('href') || link.href)).filter(Boolean); return links[0] || ''; }; const textOf = (article) => normalize(Array.from(article.querySelectorAll("div[data-testid='tweetText'], div[lang]")).map((node) => normalize(node.textContent)).filter(Boolean).join('\n\n')); const buttonText = (article, selector) => { const el = article.querySelector(selector); return el ? normalize(el.textContent) : null; }; const posts = []; const seen = new Set(); for (const article of Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("main article[data-testid='tweet'], main [data-testid='cellInnerDiv'] article, main article"))) { const postUrl = getStatusUrl(article); if (!postUrl || seen.has(postUrl)) continue; seen.add(postUrl); posts.push({ post_url: postUrl, author: normalize(article.querySelector("div[data-testid='User-Name']")?.textContent || ''), text: textOf(article), posted_at: article.querySelector('time')?.getAttribute('datetime') || null, reply_count: buttonText(article, "button[data-testid='reply'], [data-testid='reply']"), repost_count: buttonText(article, "button[data-testid='retweet'], button[data-testid='unretweet'], [data-testid='retweet'], [data-testid='unretweet']"), like_count: buttonText(article, "button[data-testid='like'], button[data-testid='unlike'], [data-testid='like'], [data-testid='unlike']"), view_count: buttonText(article, "a[href*='/analytics']") }); if (posts.length >= 30) break; } return { url: location.href, post_count: posts.length, posts };

help.summary: Return a digest of posts currently visible on the authenticated X home timeline.

examples.description: Scroll the home feed and return a compact post digest.

help.returns: { url, post_count, posts[] } where each post has post_url, author, text, posted_at, reply_count, repost_count, like_count, view_count.
notes: Opens a fresh tab so parallel runs do not fight over the same window.
Requires an already-logged-in x.com session in the browser.
Read-only: no likes, follows, or posts are ever issued.
